title: 7 Exception Items
body_text:
7	Exception Items
7.1	Overview
This Chapter provides the procedures for handling disputed PADs. This includes procedures for the following:
Completing the Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701
Requesting a copy of a reimbursement claim of PAD form
Completing the 芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs, Form 2065
This Chapter also lists the AFT Transactions ineligible for return via the Clearing and outlines the procedures for handling rejected return transactions.
Related Information
Reimbursement Claim for a PAD,
Form 1701
芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs,
Form 2065
7.2	Handling Disputed PADs
The Payor (the member), may dispute a PAD and make a claim for reimbursement under the following conditions:
The PAD wasn芒聙聶t drawn in accordance with the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696 (or equivalent agreement).
The Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696 (or equivalent agreement), was revoked.
Pre-notification of the PAD wasn芒聙聶t received according to CPA Rule H1. Unless Pre-notification is waived under the terms of the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696, or a separate waiver, the Payor must be notified of fixed amount PADs at least 10 calendar days before the first PAD and every time there is a change in the amount or payment date. For variable amount PADs, the Payor must be notified at least ten calendar days before each transaction.
A Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696 (or equivalent agreement), or an agreement for a Cash Management PAD didn芒聙聶t exist between the Payor and the Payee. This applies to all types of PADs including Funds Transfer PAD (code 650 and 83) and Cash Management PADs (code 420 or 44).
The originating financial institution (which holds the account of the Payee) will honour and provide recourse for PADs returned according to the procedures in this Section. If the Payee disputes the claim, see Payee Disputes Member芒聙聶s Claim for Reimbursement, below.
Note:	If there is a reason to believe that the Payee will try to debit the Payor芒聙聶s account again, the Payor can request a stop payment on the PAD. Stop payments are available if the financial institution芒聙聶s banking system facilitates stop payment requests for PADs. For procedures on setting up a stop payment, see Chapter 7, Stop Payment Requests for PADs, in the AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part.
Claims Made within the CPA Recourse Time Frames
Proceed as follows to process a reimbursement request for a PAD if it is made within the CPA芒聙聶s specified timelines as summarized in Section 1.3, Recourse Deadlines:
Obtain a signed Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701, or a recorded equivalent from the member. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701
Retain a copy of the completed and signed form or the recorded claim for a minimum of 12 months.
Note:	If a financial institution is unable to process its own US dollar returns, circle the item on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (UCD). Forward the Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701, and the UCD to Central 1芒聙聶s AFT Desk, Payment Services. Central 1 will process the return on the financial institution芒聙聶s behalf. See Section 1.3, Recourse Deadlines. Retain a copy of the form or the recording for 12 months.
Reimburse the member for the claim.
Return the PAD to the originating financial institution via the Online Return System (ORS). For procedures, see the Online Return System (ORS) Part.
Requesting a Copy or Recording of the Reimbursement Claim of PAD
Financial institutions can request a copy of a signed or recorded reimbursement claim for a PAD from a financial institution via Central 1. To request a copy of a signed or recorded reimbursement claim for a PAD, complete the Request for Reimbursement Claim, Form 2425, and fax to Central 1 as indicated on the form. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Request for Reimbursement Claim, Form 2425
Note:	As per CPA rules, the Payor芒聙聶s financial institution provides either a signed copy or a recorded message of the reimbursement claim for a PAD or a reimbursement settlement within 30 calendar days of receipt of the Request for Reimbursement Claim, Form 2425.
Claims Made outside the CPA Recourse Time Frames
Determine if the claim for reimbursement has been made outside of the CPA芒聙聶s recourse time frames.
See the table in Section 1.3, Recourse Deadlines.
Check the 芒聙聹Reason芒聙聺 column to find the situation specific to the member.
Determine if the transaction is a business or personal/Funds Transfer PAD and see the 芒聙聹Time Frame for Return芒聙聺 column.
Advise the member to speak directly to the Payee if the claim for reimbursement is made outside of the published time frame. These PADs can芒聙聶t be returned through the Clearing. See Section 1.3, Recourse Deadlines.
Payee Disputes Member芒聙聶s Claim for Reimbursement
A PAD is returned if the Payor, the member, claims one of the following:
The transaction wasn芒聙聶t processed in accordance with the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696.
The agreement was revoked.
Pre-notification wasn芒聙聶t received.
There was no Payor芒聙聶s PAD or alternate agreement in place.
However, if the Payee disputes the validity of the member芒聙聶s claim, as set out in the Reimbursement Claim for a PAD, Form 1701, the parties must handle the dispute outside of the Clearing.
Reimbursement for Funds Transfer PAD Disputes
If there is a reimbursement claim for a PAD coded 650 or 83, the parties must deal with it outside the Clearing. If you didn芒聙聶t reimburse the Payor (the member) for the disputed Funds Transfer PAD, complete the 芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs, Form 2065. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: 芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs, Form 2065
Provide the form to the member and advise them to present it to the originating financial institution for reimbursement. The purpose of the form is to indicate that the Payor (the member) hasn芒聙聶t already received reimbursement from the financial institution.
Exception:	Where the Payor disputes a 芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs, Form 2065, because no Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement exists with the Payee, the PAD can be returned through the Clearing as described earlier in this Section.
Related Information
Section 1.3
, Recourse Deadlines
Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Reimbursement Claim for a PAD,
Form 1701
芒聙聵No Reimbursement芒聙聶 for Funds Transfer PADs,
Form 2065
Request for Reimbursement Claim,
Form 2425
7.3	AFT Transactions Ineligible for Return via the Clearing
The following types of AFT Transactions aren芒聙聶t eligible for return via the Clearing:
Returned Transactions
芒聙聯
Returned Credit Transactions (I records) and returned Debit Transactions (J records) can芒聙聶t be returned through the Clearing. In other words, you can芒聙聶t return a return. Pursue resolution outside the Clearing.
Funds Transfer PADs
芒聙聯 If the financial institution elects not to offer recourse on disputed Funds Transfer PADs, where permissible, create them as code 650 for electronic transactions or code 83 for paper transactions. If the financial institution chooses to allow recourse, create the transaction under a different code.
If there is a reimbursement claim for a disputed PAD coded 650 or 83, the parties must deal with it outside the Clearing unless it is disputed for the reason that no Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement exists with the Payee.
However, a dishonoured transaction or a return of an Error Correction can be processed through the Clearing even if it is a Funds Transfer PAD. To ensure that the rules surrounding both scenarios are followed, see Section 6.2, Returning Dishonoured AFT Transactions.
Cash Management PADs
芒聙聯 If there is a reimbursement claim for a disputed PAD coded 420 or 44, the parties must deal with it outside the Clearing unless it is disputed because no Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement exists with the Payee.
However, a dishonoured transaction or a return of an Error Correction can be processed through the Clearing even if it is a Cash Management PAD. To ensure that the rules surrounding both scenarios are followed, see Section 6.2, Returning Dishonoured AFT Transactions.
Offsetting Entry in an AFT File Originated by the Financial Institution
芒聙聯 You can芒聙聶t return an offsetting entry in the original AFT file because it will always come back to you, the Originator. ORS doesn芒聙聶t prevent users from returning these transactions, but the request will generate two transactions that cancel each other out. Financial institution staff can do this when
芒聢聮
an offsetting Debit Transaction for a credit file can芒聙聶t be posted because the member芒聙聶s account is overdrawn;
芒聢聮
an offsetting Credit Transaction for a debit file can芒聙聶t be posted because the member芒聙聶s account is closed.
To ensure that you don芒聙聶t accidentally return this type of transaction, check the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report. If the 芒聙聹Return Bank Address芒聙聺 specifies the financial institution, you must contact the member directly to settle the outage.
ICBC Agent Deposit Account Transactions (BC only)
芒聙聯 You can芒聙聶t return this type of transaction because it will Reject and appear on the Batch Returns Error List (ICBC/UCBE). Contact Central 1 to process these items.
Related Information
Section 6.2
, Returning Dishonoured AFT Transactions
Section 7.2
, Handing Disputed PADs
7.4	Handling Rejected Return Transactions
Once an AFT file or batch is successfully received at Central 1, a transaction can Reject if
the return reason code is invalid;
the return reference number is invalid;
it has already been returned or recalled;
it is an ICBC Agent Deposit Account transaction.
Note:	See Section 7.3, AFT Transactions Ineligible for Return via the Clearing.
Rejected transactions don芒聙聶t cause the entire file or batch to Reject. Proceed as follows to identify and process rejected returns.
Note:	The following reports are available in the MERG File via Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing reports.
Check the following report daily:
芒聢聮
Batch Returns Error List (ICBE/UCBE)
芒聙聯 Lists transactions rejected during Central 1芒聙聶s initial edit.
Determine the reason for rejection by referring to the Batch Returns Error List (ICBE/UCBE).
Use the dollar amount, reference number to match the rejected return to the original transaction on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD). The CCD/UCD can be from 2 to 3 days earlier.
Reprocess the return as follows:
芒聢聮
If the transaction rejected due to a keying error (e.g., the amount, reference number or return code was keyed incorrectly), re-enter it on the returned item software with the correct information.
芒聢聮
If the transaction rejected because it was a duplicate, investigate and confirm that the transaction was already returned.
芒聢聮
If the transaction rejected because it was an ICBC Agent Deposit Account transaction, contact Central 1. See Section 7.3, AFT Transactions Ineligible for Return via the Clearing.
Related Information
Section 7.3
, AFT Transactions Ineligible for Return via the Clearing
END
